title: S6a: workflow-state — repair the duplicate rule ids, pin, and migrate the FIRST cluster group
action: The last area, and the largest: docs/specs/workflow-state.md, 1464 lines, ~140 anchors. Per F10 it is SPLIT ACROSS CELLS — this cell does the repair, the pin, and the first group only. HAZARD FIRST: the source carries DUPLICATE rule ids R19/R20/R21 (at roughly lines 891-899 and again 916-925, found by the f2-4 sweep). Anchors are keyed by id, so the first member of each pair is overwritten and becomes unmeasurable by the fidelity floor forever. Resolve exactly as hook-runtime's R14 was resolved in commit 687ac59 — read that commit first, it is the precedent: read both blocks of each pair, confirm they are genuinely distinct rules, then renumber the occurrence whose id is cited by FEWER live references (grep skills/ scripts/ hooks/ .bee/bin/ AGENTS.md docs/specs/ for each id before choosing) using a disambiguation suffix, BEFORE the pin is captured — so the pinned blob already carries distinct ids. Quote both blocks of all three pairs and the citation counts that decided each direction. If any pair turns out to be one rule stated twice, say so and treat it as one. THEN: STEP 0 capture the pin (scheme ba-nine-section, full expected_counts including unparsed_blocks, verbatim source copy hash-verified — note hook-runtime's repaired-source pin form if the repair means no commit holds the bytes). STEP 1 author ONLY the first cluster group of okf-foundation D30's locked map: overview (Purpose+Entry Points+Data Dictionary+Actors) and gates (B1, B2, B9a, B19, the Closing-a-feature section). Leave every other anchor unclaimed for the follow-up cells — the gate WILL be red on those, which is expected and is why the chain entry is added by the LAST cell of this group, not this one. STEP 2/3 deferred to the final workflow-state cell. Record in the trace exactly which anchors this cell claimed and which remain, so the next cell starts from a precise ledger.

truths: node scripts/run_verify.mjs exits 0 — the chain must stay green, which means the workflow-state gate is NOT wired in this cell (it will be red until the last group lands)
all three duplicate pairs resolved BEFORE the pin: both blocks of each quoted, with the live-citation counts that decided which side was renumbered
derived ids are all distinct after the repair — quote the before/after distinct-id count
the pin is captured and verifiable: blob_sha matches the committed source copy via git hash-object
the concepts authored in this cell pass the fidelity floor individually — min/median quoted for the anchors they claim
knowledge check 0 errors 0 warnings; index --check 0 stale
the trace records the exact anchor ledger: claimed by this cell vs remaining
prohibitions: never migrate while two anchors share an id
never drop or merge a rule to remove a duplicate
never renumber the side with MORE live citations
never invent ids for unnumbered blocks (D10)
do not wire the workflow-state chain entry in this cell — it belongs to the last group cell
never edit a concept or lower a threshold to clear the floor

trace.outcome: workflow-state: three duplicate rule ids (R19/R20/R21) repaired to R19a/R20a/R21a before pinning, pin captured (140 anchors, 140 distinct, blob 506fef9, repaired_from ed1644c), first cluster group authored (overview + gates, 4 anchors, fidelity 1.000); chain green at 74 suites
deviations: docs/knowledge/work/okf-migration-f2/ (work-item.md + its generated index.md) was NOT authored by this cell and is not in its files list, but it was untracked in the checkout when the cell ran, so `bee knowledge index` folded it into docs/knowledge/index.md and docs/knowledge/work/index.md. Committing those regenerated indexes without it would leave a commit whose `bee knowledge index --check` is red on checkout, so the two files are carried in this commit unchanged. .bee/backlog.jsonl, .bee/decisions.jsonl and .bee/cells/f2-11.json were also dirty on arrival and are deliberately left for the orchestrator's own bookkeeping.

CONSTRAINT READING: "docs/history/** untouched" is honoured except for the two paths the cell's own files list names — docs/history/okf-migration-f2/sources/workflow-state.md (the pin's committed content address, required by F8) and sources/README.md (its registry row). Same reading as cells f2-10 and f2-11.

The workflow-state chain gate (`okf_migrate --check workflow-state`) is deliberately NOT wired and the pointer stub is deliberately NOT written: F10 splits this area across cells, 136 of 140 anchors are still unclaimed, and the gate belongs to the LAST group cell. The chain therefore stays at 74 suites, not 75.
trace.verification_evidence: {"hazard_repair_three_duplicate_ids": {"before": "node scripts/okf_migrate.mjs --inventory <provenance blob ed1644c> -> 140 anchors, 137 DISTINCT; duplicated ids exactly ['R19','R20','R21'] (counts {behaviors:37,rules:58,edges:25,pointers:20,total:140} added up perfectly the whole time)", "after": "node scripts/okf_migrate.mjs --inventory docs/specs/workflow-state.md -> 140 anchors, 140 DISTINCT, dups []; ids present R19,R20,R21,R19a,R20a,R21a; counts and unparsed_blocks unchanged (7)", "citation_counts": "grep -rn '\\bR19\\b|\\bR20\\b|\\bR21\\b' skills/ scripts/ hooks/ .bee/bin/ AGENTS.md docs/specs/ -> ZERO citations of any workflow-state rule id on EITHER side (hits are onboarding.md's and hook-runtime's own unrelated R19/R20/R21, plus workflow-state.md's own six definition lines). grep -rno 'workflow-state[.a-z]*#R[0-9]*[a-z]*' over the same trees -> 0 matches. Tie broken on document order per the 687ac59 R14 precedent; the only surviving external mention, docs/history/fresh-session-handoff/reports/validation-s5.md ('workflow-state.md B15/B16/R19-R21'), means the FIRST (kept) family", "renamed_side": "chain-integrity family (second occurrence in document order, L916/L921/L925) -> R19a/R20a/R21a; three tokens on three lines, `git diff --stat docs/specs/workflow-state.md` = 3 insertions(+), 3 deletions(-)"}, "pin": "node scripts/okf_migrate.mjs --verify-pins exit 0: PASS workflow-state: 140 anchors {\"behaviors\":37,\"rules\":58,\"edges\":25,\"pointers\":20,\"total\":140,\"unparsed_blocks\":7} from blob 506fef94814b9197706c6dcab9a09e56941609d4 via committed-copy (scheme ba-nine-section); repaired_from ed1644c487043b0373b0ceea727143d4ddbfa3e8 at df3072d:docs/specs/workflow-state.md. git hash-object docs/history/okf-migration-f2/sources/workflow-state.md = 506fef94814b9197706c6dcab9a09e56941609d4 (matches blob_sha)", "strict_no_op_all_ten_prior_pins": "--verify-pins green across all ELEVEN pins with every pre-existing expected_counts byte-unchanged: advisor-protocol 26 {4,9,6,7,0} | doctrine-layer 39 {10,17,5,7,2} | critical-patterns 47 {47,0} | decision-memory 9 {0,9,0,0,0} | verify-pipeline 14 {0,5,4,5,7} | performance-log 23 {0,11,5,7,10} | feedback-digest 29 {0,15,6,8,26} | onboarding 58 {0,28,15,15,20} | hook-runtime 81 {22,24,17,18,8} | worktree-parallelism 10 {10,10,2}; test_okf_pins section 28's FROZEN table re-asserted green", "fidelity": "node scripts/okf_migrate.mjs --fidelity workflow-state -> n=4 (B1,B2,B9a,B19), min 1.000, median 1.000, max 1.000, floor 0.60 — verbatim re-homing, no concept edited and no threshold lowered", "coverage_partial_by_design": "node scripts/okf_migrate.mjs --check workflow-state -> FAIL: 140 anchors, 4 owned, 0 duplicated, 136 lost. EXPECTED and NOT wired into the chain: F10 splits this area across cells and the gate belongs to the LAST group cell", "red_first": {"claim_removal": "with `docs/specs/workflow-state.md#B9a` removed from gates.md's bee.sources, --check workflow-state reported 'LOST in concepts: B9a is claimed by no concept's bee.sources (expected one owner)' and owned dropped 4 -> 3 (137 lost); restored to 4 owned / 136 lost", "repair_declaration": "test_okf_pins section 30 asserts the pin WITHOUT repaired_from/repair_reason, with a wrong repaired_from, and with a blank repair_reason are all refused PIN_SHA_MISMATCH"}, "bundle_invariants": "bee knowledge check --json -> okf errors [], profile warnings [] (both new concepts normalized through emitFrontmatter after an initial not_canonical finding); bee knowledge index --check -> 16 expected index file(s), 0 stale", "telemetry": "workflow-state anchors_per_concept 70.00, concepts_per_100_source_lines 0.14 (140 anchors, 2 concepts, 1465 source lines) — mid-migration by construction; it shifts the ba-nine-section medians only from 7.125 -> 7.25 and 1.538 -> 1.504, and NO other area falls outside [0.5x, 2x] (test_okf_pins green, all 74 chain suites green)", "suite": "node scripts/test_okf_pins.mjs -> PASS, 418 assertions (was 397; section 30 added for the workflow-state triple repair)", "chain": "node scripts/run_verify.mjs -> PASS run_verify: 74 suite(s), concurrency=5, wall=62015ms (exit 0). Baseline before any edit was the same 74 suites green", "red_failure_evidence": "THE PRIOR BEHAVIOR, measured not asserted. Before this cell, docs/specs/workflow-state.md could not be pinned honestly: `node scripts/okf_migrate.mjs --inventory` against the provenance blob (git show df3072d:docs/specs/workflow-state.md, blob ed1644c) derives 140 anchors carrying only 137 DISTINCT ids — duplicated ids exactly ['R19','R20','R21'] — while every declared count {behaviors:37,rules:58,edges:25,pointers:20,total:140,unparsed_blocks:7} added up perfectly, so no count-based guard could ever see it. Anchors are keyed by id in a Map, so each first member's text was overwritten by its second: three rules permanently unmeasurable by the F11 fidelity floor and invisible to set-equality. test_okf_pins section 30 re-measures that exact prior state from the provenance blob on every run (140 anchors / 137 distinct / collisions exactly R19,R20,R21), so the before-state is asserted in the chain, not just described here. Second prior behavior: `node scripts/okf_migrate.mjs --check workflow-state` did not exist as a derivable check at all — there was no workflow-state pin, so the area had no ground truth. Red-first on the new surface: with the #B9a claim removed from gates.md, --check reported 'LOST in concepts: B9a is claimed by no concept's bee.sources' and owned fell 4 -> 3; restored to 4 owned. Red-first on the repaired-pin branch: the workflow-state pin with repaired_from/repair_reason deleted, with a wrong repaired_from, and with a blank repair_reason are each refused PIN_SHA_MISMATCH (test_okf_pins section 30)."}
